name: wasp-config
author: wasp
//build: tsc outputs files without executable permissions. run.js is a bin entry
and must be executable. npm only chmod's bin files when it creates the
.bin/ symlink, not on subsequent installs. In our case, waspc's ts-setup
deletes and re-copies .wasp/wasp-config/ but the node_modules/.bin/
symlink survives, so npm skips the chmod. The chmod +x here ensures the
source in data/packages/ is executable, which copyDirRecur preserves.
//vitest: We pin `vitest` version because type tests are an experimental feature,
and changes to API are not breaking changes.
